title: EigenLayer Restaking - Open App
meta_description: eigenlayer restaking: open the official app to stake ETH, LSTs, or EIGEN, delegate to an operator, and review risks before signing.
hero_h2: Stake assets to help secure verifiable services
hero_lead: EigenLayer Restaking routes ETH, LST, and EIGEN holders to the official app for deposits, operator delegation, reward visibility, and risk review.
what_is: EigenLayer is an Ethereum protocol that introduces restaking so users can extend cryptoeconomic security to Autonomous Verifiable Services, as described in the official EigenLayer docs. The app supports liquid restaking flows and native ETH restaking paths with operator delegation and documented slashing considerations.

how.d: Go to app.eigenlayer.xyz on Ethereum Mainnet and connect a Web3 wallet you control.
Use the token view for supported liquid assets, or follow the native ETH path if you operate an Ethereum validator.
Delegate available restaked balance to an operator; EigenLayer documents delegation as a single-operator, all-or-nothing action.
Review token approvals, deposit and delegation prompts, then confirm transactions in your wallet.

points.p: The restaking user guide points users to the EigenLayer Web App for liquid restaking on Ethereum Mainnet.
Liquid restaking covers supported assets shown in the app, including LSTs, EIGEN, and ERC-20 strategies; native ETH restaking uses an EigenPod.
Delegated stake can become slashable depending on operator and AVS activity, so review operator allocations, withdrawal timing, and wallet permissions before signing.

faq.a: eigenlayer restaking is EigenLayer's model for depositing native ETH, liquid staking tokens, EIGEN, or supported ERC-20 strategies and delegating them to operators that help secure AVSs.

faq.q: How do I start eigenlayer restaking in the official app?
faq.a: Use app.eigenlayer.xyz, connect your wallet on Ethereum Mainnet, choose an asset, submit any required approval, and confirm deposit or delegation transactions.

faq.q: Does eigenlayer restaking require an operator?
faq.a: For delegation, yes. EigenLayer docs state that stakers can delegate to a single operator at a time and that delegation applies to all available restaked balance.

faq.q: What assets can be used for eigenlayer restaking?
faq.a: The liquid path includes supported assets displayed in the app, such as LSTs, EIGEN, and ERC-20 strategies; native ETH restaking requires validator operations and an EigenPod.

faq.q: What risks should I review before eigenlayer restaking?
faq.a: Review slashing exposure, operator reputation, AVS allocations, withdrawal delays, and token approval permissions before signing any transaction.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify app.eigenlayer.xyz before signing. Educational only, not financial advice.
